title: PRD-018: Quality and Testing Infrastructure

overview.description: Establish comprehensive testing infrastructure that ensures reliability through high code coverage, property-based testing, fuzz testing, mutation testing, benchmarks, and robust CI integration.
goals: 80%+ unit test coverage across all modules
Property-based testing for config parsing and version matching
Fuzz testing for TOML parser edge cases
Mutation testing to verify test effectiveness
Benchmark suite to prevent performance regressions
Code coverage reporting in CI with PR integration
Test containers for realistic integration testing

userStories.title: Unit Test Coverage Target (80%+)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want 80%+ unit test coverage enforced in CI, so that new code is thoroughly tested before merging.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Coverage measured using cargo-llvm-cov or tarpaulin
Coverage threshold of 80% enforced in CI (PR fails if below)
Coverage badge displayed in README
Module-level coverage breakdown available
Uncovered lines highlighted in PR comments (via codecov/coveralls)

userStories.title: Integration Tests with Real Tool Installation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want integration tests that actually install tools, so that I can verify the full installation flow works correctly.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Integration test suite exists in tests/integration/
Tests install real tools (jq, ripgrep, etc.) in isolated environments
CI caching for installed tools to speed up subsequent runs
Platform-specific tests (macOS, Ubuntu, Fedora, Windows)
Tests run in containers/VMs for isolation
#[ignore] attribute for tests that require real package managers
CI matrix runs integration tests on all supported platforms
Test cleanup removes installed tools after test completion

userStories.title: Property-Based Testing for Config Parsing
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want property-based tests for config parsing, so that the parser handles arbitrary valid inputs correctly.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: proptest or quickcheck crate added as dev-dependency
Generate arbitrary valid TOML configurations
Verify parsing round-trips correctly (parse -> serialize -> parse)
Test version string parsing with random semver-like inputs
Test tool spec parsing with random package manager combinations
Minimum 1000 test cases per property
Properties documented in test files

userStories.title: Fuzz Testing for TOML Parser Edge Cases
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want fuzz testing for the config parser, so that malformed inputs don't crash or cause undefined behavior.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: cargo-fuzz with libfuzzer set up
Fuzz target for jarvy.toml parsing (full config)
Fuzz target for version string parsing
Fuzz target for tool spec parsing
Fuzz target for hook script validation
Corpus of interesting inputs maintained
CI runs short fuzz sessions (60 seconds) on every PR
Weekly scheduled fuzz runs (8+ hours)
Crashes/panics automatically create GitHub issues

userStories.title: Mutation Testing to Verify Test Quality
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want mutation testing to verify test effectiveness, so that I know tests actually catch real bugs.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: cargo-mutants configured for mutation testing
Mutation score target of 60%+ (mutations caught / total mutations)
Weekly scheduled mutation testing runs in CI
Report identifies tests that don't catch mutations
Critical modules (config.rs, common.rs) require 70%+ mutation score
Mutation testing documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md

userStories.title: Benchmark Suite for Performance Regression
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want benchmarks for critical paths, so that I can detect performance regressions before they ship.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: criterion crate added for benchmarks
Benchmark for config file parsing (small, medium, large files)
Benchmark for tool registry lookup
Benchmark for version comparison operations
Benchmark for parallel installation orchestration
Baseline stored in repository
CI compares PR benchmarks against baseline
Alert on >10% performance regression
Benchmark results posted as PR comments

userStories.title: Code Coverage Reporting in CI
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want code coverage integrated with GitHub PRs, so that I can see coverage impact of every change.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Coverage uploaded to Codecov or Coveralls
PR comments show coverage diff
Coverage trend graphs in dashboard
Branch coverage in addition to line coverage
Configurable coverage thresholds per module
Coverage report excludes test code and generated code

functionalRequirements.description: 80%+ unit test coverage enforced in CI
Property-based testing for config and version parsing
Fuzz testing for TOML parser with 4+ fuzz targets
Code coverage reporting integrated with PRs
Test containers for Ubuntu, Fedora, Alpine
Integration tests on macOS, Linux, Windows
Mock package manager layer for unit testing

nonGoals: Testing external package managers (apt, brew, winget)

risks.mitigation: Parallelize, use caching, run long tests on schedule
Isolated containers, retry logic, deterministic ordering
Run weekly, limit to critical modules
